   712→def parse_bench(text: str, filename: Optional[str] = None) -> Circuit:
   713→    """Parse circuit from BENCH format."""
   714→    circuit = Circuit()
   715→    seen_signals = set()
   716→    lines = text.split('\n')
   717→
   718→    # First pass: collect all INPUT and OUTPUT declarations
   719→    for line_num, line in enumerate(lines, 1):
   720→        line = line.strip()
   721→        # Remove comments
   722→        if '#' in line:
   723→            line = line[:line.index('#')]
   724→        line = line.strip()
   725→        if not line:
   726→            continue
   727→
   728→        # Parse INPUT(name)
   729→        if line.startswith('INPUT(') and line.endswith(')'):
   730→            name = line[6:-1].strip()
   731→            if not name:
   732→                raise BenchParseError(f"Empty INPUT name at line {line_num}", filename, line_num, 1)
   733→            # Check for brackets in identifier (scalar-only constraint)
   734→            if '[' in name or ']' in name:
   735→                raise BenchParseError(f"Invalid identifier '{name}': brackets not allowed in BENCH format", filename, line_num, 1)
   736→            if name in seen_signals:
   737→                raise RedefinitionError(f"Signal '{name}' defined multiple times", filename)
   738→            si = SignalInfo(name, 0, 0)
   739→            circuit.inputs.append(si)
   740→            circuit.all_signals[name] = si
   741→            seen_signals.add(name)
   742→            continue
   743→
   744→        # Parse OUTPUT(name)
   745→        if line.startswith('OUTPUT(') and line.endswith(')'):
   746→            name = line[7:-1].strip()
   747→            if not name:
   748→                raise BenchParseError(f"Empty OUTPUT name at line {line_num}", filename, line_num, 1)
   749→            if '[' in name or ']' in name:
   750→                raise BenchParseError(f"Invalid identifier '{name}': brackets not allowed in BENCH format", filename, line_num, 1)
   751→            if name in seen_signals:
   752→                raise RedefinitionError(f"Signal '{name}' defined multiple times", filename)
   753→            si = SignalInfo(name, 0, 0)
   754→            circuit.outputs.append(si)
   755→            circuit.all_signals[name] = si
   756→            seen_signals.add(name)
   757→            continue
   758→
   759→        # Parse assignment: lhs = OP(arg1, arg2, ...)
   760→        if '=' in line:
   761→            parts = line.split('=', 1)
   762→            if len(parts) != 2:
   763→                raise BenchParseError(f"Invalid assignment syntax at line {line_num}", filename, line_num, 1)
   764→            lhs = parts[0].strip()
   765→            rhs = parts[1].strip()
   766→
   767→            if not lhs:
   768→                raise BenchParseError(f"Empty LHS in assignment at line {line_num}", filename, line_num, 1)
   769→            if '[' in lhs or ']' in lhs:
   770→                raise BenchParseError(f"Invalid identifier '{lhs}': brackets not allowed in BENCH format", filename, line_num, 1)
   771→
   772→            # Check for literals in RHS (BENCH constraint: no literals)
   773→            # Look for 0, 1, 0b..., 0x..., etc. as standalone tokens
   774→            # Simple check: if RHS contains digits not part of operator names
   775→            rhs_stripped = rhs.strip()
   776→            # Check for numeric literals (starting with digit or 0b/0x)
   777→            import re
   778→            if re.match(r'^\d', rhs_stripped) or rhs_stripped.startswith(('0b', '0B', '0x', '0X')):
   779→                raise BenchParseError(f"Literals not allowed in BENCH format: '{rhs_stripped}'", filename, line_num, 1)
   780→
   781→            # Parse operator call
   782→            match = re.match(r'^([A-Z]+)\((.+)\)$', rhs_stripped)
   783→            if not match:
   784→                raise BenchParseError(f"Invalid operator syntax: '{rhs_stripped}' at line {line_num}", filename, line_num, 1)
   785→
   786→            op = match.group(1)
   787→            args_str = match.group(2)
   788→
   789→            # Supported operators
   790→            unary_ops = {'NOT', 'BUF'}
   791→            variadic_ops = {'AND', 'OR', 'XOR', 'NAND', 'NOR', 'XNOR'}
   792→
   793→            if op not in unary_ops and op not in variadic_ops:
   794→                raise BenchParseError(f"Unsupported operator '{op}' in BENCH format", filename, line_num, 1)
   795→
   796→            # Parse arguments (comma-separated identifiers)
   797→            args = [a.strip() for a in args_str.split(',')]
   798→            args = [a for a in args if a]  # remove empty
   799→
   800→            if not args:
   801→                raise BenchParseError(f"Operator '{op}' requires at least one argument", filename, line_num, 1)
   802→
   803→            # Check for brackets in arguments (scalar-only)
   804→            for arg in args:
   805→                if '[' in arg or ']' in arg:
   806→                    raise BenchParseError(f"Invalid identifier '{arg}': brackets not allowed in BENCH format", filename, line_num, 1)
   807→
   808→            # Check arity
   809→            if op in unary_ops and len(args) != 1:
   810→                raise arity_error(op, 1, len(args))
   811→            # variadic ops require at least 2 args
   812→            if op in variadic_ops and len(args) < 2:
   813→                raise arity_error(op, '>=2', len(args))
   814→
   815→            # Check LHS is valid
   816→            if lhs not in seen_signals:
   817→                raise undefined_name(lhs)
   818→            if lhs in circuit.inputs:
   819→                raise input_assignment(lhs)
   820→            if lhs in circuit.assignments:
   821→                raise multiple_assignment(lhs)
   822→
   823→            # Check all arguments are defined (either inputs or previously assigned wires)
   824→            for arg in args:
   825→                if arg not in circuit.all_signals:
   826→                    # In BENCH, wires are implicitly declared by assignment
   827→                    # but OUTPUT references must reference defined signals
   828→                    # For now, we'll track that args are used as identifiers
   829→                    pass
   830→
   831→            # Create expression node for the RHS
   832→            expr_args = []
   833→            for arg in args:
   834→                # Check if argument refers to undefined signal
   835→                if arg not in circuit.all_signals:
   836→                    # Implicit wire declaration - add it now
   837→                    if arg in seen_signals:
   838→                        raise RedefinitionError(f"Signal '{arg}' defined multiple times", filename)
   839→                    arg_si = SignalInfo(arg, 0, 0)
   840→                    circuit.all_signals[arg] = arg_si
   841→                    seen_signals.add(arg)
   842→                expr_args.append(ExprNode(NodeType.IDENTIFIER, line_num, 1, arg))
   843→
   844→            expr = ExprNode(NodeType.CALL, line_num, 1, op, expr_args)
   845→            circuit.assignments[lhs] = expr
   846→
   847→    # After parsing, validate OUTPUT references
   848→    for out_sig in circuit.outputs:
   849→        if out_sig.name not in circuit.assignments:
   850→            raise undefined_name(out_sig.name)
   851→
   852→    # Validate all assignments' arguments refer to existing signals
   853→    for lhs, expr in list(circuit.assignments.items()):
   854→        for arg_expr in _get_identifiers(expr):
   855→            if arg_expr not in circuit.all_signals:
   856→                # This shouldn't happen with our parsing above, but check anyway
   857→                raise undefined_name(arg_expr)
   858→
   859→    # Check for cycles (same as in validate function)
   860→    visited = set(); rec = set(); path = []
   861→    def dfs(n):
   862→        visited.add(n); rec.add(n); path.append(n)
   863→        if n in circuit.assignments:
   864→            for dep in _get_identifiers(circuit.assignments[n]):
   865→                if dep not in circuit.all_signals: continue
   866→                if dep not in visited:
   867→                    if dfs(dep): return True
   868→                elif dep in rec:
   869→                    raise cycle_error(path[path.index(dep):] + [dep])
   870→        path.pop(); rec.remove(n); return False
   871→    for sig in circuit.outputs + list(circuit.assignments.keys()):
   872→        if sig not in visited and dfs(sig): return
   873→
   874→    return circuit
   875→
   876→
   877→def _get_identifiers(expr: ExprNode) -> list:
   878→    if expr.node_type == NodeType.IDENTIFIER: return [expr.value]
   879→    if expr.node_type == NodeType.LITERAL: return []
   880→    if expr.node_type == NodeType.CALL: return [i for a in expr.args for i in _get_identifiers(a)]
   881→    return []
